key_points: Widely used Python backtesting library for trading strategies with a large ecosystem and lots of community examples.
caveats: Topic-page activity marker was Aug 2024, so it looks more mature/stable than cutting-edge.

key_points: Simple, popular Python backtest framework and a strong default starting point for stock-like strategies.
caveats: Options are not the main focus; best fit is stock/bar-data style testing.

key_points: Lightning-fast engine that is especially good for research notebooks, parameter sweeps, and rapid signal iteration.
caveats: More research-oriented than beginner tutorial-oriented.

key_points: Explicit support for stocks, options, futures, forex, and live trading workflows makes it one of the more practical all-around picks.
caveats: Broader platform scope means more setup than a minimal library.

key_points: Advanced event-driven backtester with data-vendor and broker integrations; supports crypto, stocks, and futures.
caveats: Feels more institutional/quant than plug-and-play.

key_points: High-star stock toolkit covering data acquisition, indicators, stock selection, strategy validation/backtesting, and auto-trading.
caveats: A lot of the ecosystem/documentation is Chinese-language.

key_points: Modular quant stack with stock-market, technical-analysis, and algorithmic-trading focus.
caveats: Broader quant framework rather than an options-first solution.

key_points: Algorithmic trading framework in Python with a strong machine-learning angle.
caveats: Not an options-first framework.

key_points: Multi-asset framework for building, backtesting, and deploying trading algorithms across stocks, crypto, and forex.
caveats: Less stock/options-specialized than the narrower picks.

key_points: Open-source portfolio backtesting engine that looks useful for allocation and portfolio-level research.
caveats: Not options-centric.

key_points: Very active quantitative platform for research, backtesting, market data, and live execution.
caveats: AI/platform scope may be overkill for simple strategy tests.

key_points: Terminal-first lab for downloading market data, backtesting Python strategies with Pine-like behavior, and optimizing SL/TP.
caveats: More of a focused strategy lab than a general-purpose framework.

discovery_source: Direct repo page + GitHub topic: backtesting (Python)
key_points: Includes 38 built-in strategies, per-leg delta targeting, trade simulation, portfolio simulation, early exits, commissions, and detailed risk metrics.
caveats: AGPL-3.0 may matter for licensing/commercial use.

key_points: Modular framework with option primitives, strangle and put-vertical examples, CSV data handlers, and monitoring CSV output.
caveats: Older Python 2.7-era project; sample data is limited and the README expects purchased iVolatility data.

key_points: Greeks-aware risk management, stock plus options allocation, optional Rust acceleration, realistic execution, and presets like strangle, iron condor, covered call, collar, and butterfly.
caveats: Smaller community than the biggest Python backtesting libraries, but highly relevant for options portfolios.

key_points: Short-term reversal research project for option setups, universe selection, staged-entry backtesting, and live-trading hooks.
caveats: Research project rather than a mainstream library.

key_points: Black-Scholes-based options backtesting simulator.
caveats: Notebook-style simulator with simplified assumptions, not a full-market microstructure backtester.

use_case: IB API options backtesting + algo trading
key_points: Connects backtesting ideas to Interactive Brokers options workflows.
caveats: Notebook/project style and broker-specific.

key_points: Systematic volatility research and backtesting for equity options.
caveats: Narrower scope and smaller project footprint.

key_points: Notebook/code repository tied to the book "Option Greeks Strategies & Backtesting in Python."
caveats: Useful as reference material, not as a production-ready framework.

key_points: Python scripts for backtesting SPX put strategies using Black-Scholes proxies.
caveats: Older repo and proxy-based modeling.

key_points: Python code and a React app for backtesting stock options strategies.
caveats: Looks more prototype-like than framework-like.

key_points: Useful sample notebook for option-strategy backtesting with LSEG/Eikon/RDP data.
caveats: Sample/article repo, not a full reusable library.

key_points: Open-source platform for designing, backtesting, and analyzing complex options strategies.
caveats: Very early-stage small community project.

key_points: Notebook covering Delta, Gamma, Theta, Vega, Rho, plus higher-order Greeks like Vomma, Charm, Vanna, and Speed with 2D/3D visualizations.
caveats: Educational notebook, not a backtesting engine.

key_points: Interactive Python toolkit for option pricing and Greeks across Black-Scholes and Monte Carlo models.
caveats: Research/education oriented and still small.

key_points: Detects IV spikes, mispricings, spread widenings, and gamma spikes; outputs CSV files and diagnostic plots.
caveats: Adjacency tool for research pipelines, not a backtesting framework.

key_points: Streamlit app that computes and visualizes implied-volatility surfaces and option Greeks.
caveats: Visualization tool, not a backtesting framework.

key_points: Real-time stock data from TradingView; useful as a JS-side data companion when prototyping.
caveats: JavaScript and primarily data-access oriented, not a Python backtester.

key_points: Toolkit for quantitative analysis of stock/crypto markets with a market screener and portfolio backtester.
caveats: JavaScript auxiliary, not Python-first.

key_points: Intraday morning-trend strategy example for backtesting NSE stocks.
caveats: Small JavaScript example repo.
